question_title: Will any party or coalition other than GERB-SDS or PP-DB win the most seats in Bulgaria's April 2026 parliamentary election before May 1, 2026?
summary: Progressive Bulgaria (PB), a brand-new coalition led by ex-President Rumen Radev (formed March 2, 2026), leads ALL polls by 5-11 percentage points over GERB-SDS, with an average lead of 8.2pp across 6 independent polling agencies. PB is projected to win ~97 seats vs GERB's ~68 out of 240 total. PP-DB polls at only 10-12%, far too low to contend for first place. PB is neither GERB-SDS nor PP-DB, so it qualifies as "other." Even with very conservative polling uncertainty assumptions (6pp combined standard deviation), GERB only overcomes the gap ~9% of the time. With standard assumptions (~4pp combined), it's about 2%. The key risk is that PB is a new party and could underperform polls, but the lead is too large and too consistent across all pollsters for GERB to realistically overtake it.

factors.description: Progressive Bulgaria leads ALL 6 polls by 5-11pp over GERB-SDS, with average lead of 8.2pp - requires ~2 sigma polling error to close
Seat projections show PB at 97 seats vs GERB at 68 (29-seat gap out of 240) on politpro.eu aggregator
PP-DB polls at only 10-12%, far too low to plausibly win most seats - effectively eliminates one of the two 'NO' parties
PB is a brand-new coalition (formed March 2, 2026) with no electoral track record - new parties have higher polling uncertainty and can underperform
Bulgarian elections have been volatile (7 snap elections since 2021) - general uncertainty in the political system
Ex-President Radev is extremely well-known nationally, reducing 'new party' risk - his personal brand drives polling support
Consistency across all pollsters (Alpha Research, Trend, Gallup International Balkan, Market Links, Sova Harris) - zero polls show GERB ahead

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.94 (94% YES)**

Progressive Bulgaria (PB), ex-President Rumen Radev's new coalition, is overwhelmingly likely to win the most seats in Bulgaria's April 19 election - making "other than GERB-SDS or PP-DB" the most probable winner. Key evidence:

- **Consistent polling lead**: PB leads GERB-SDS by 5-11pp across all 6 major polls (average 8.2pp)
- **Seat projections**: PB ~97 seats vs GERB ~68 seats (out of 240)
- **PP-DB not competitive**: At ~10-12%, PP-DB cannot win most seats
- **Cross-pollster consensus**: Alpha Research, Trend, Gallup International Balkan, Market Links, Sova Harris all show PB ahead

The main downside risk is that PB is a brand-new party (formed March 2, 2026) and could underperform polls - but even with very conservative error assumptions, the gap is too large for GERB to realistically close. The Monte Carlo simulation and z-score analysis both converge on ~94% probability.
sources_consulted: [Polls: Five groups certain to win seats in Bulgaria’s April 2026 parliamentary elections](https://sofiaglobe.com/2026/03/24/polls-five-groups-certain-to-win-seats-in-bulgarias-april-2026-parliamentary-elections/)

[Βουλγαρία: Δημοσκοπήσεις δείχνουν προβάδισμα του Radev και εύθραυστη πολυκομματική ισορροπία - IBNA Bulgaria Polls 2026: Progressive Bulgaria Leads GERB-SDS Ahead of April Elections](https://www.ibnaeu.com/en/2026/04/03/bulgaria-elections-2026-polls-progressive-bulgaria-gerb/)

[Latest Survey: “Progressive Bulgaria” Surges Ahead in April Elections Projection, GERB Trails Far Behind - Novinite.com - Sofia News Agency](https://www.novinite.com/articles/237631/Latest+Survey:+%E2%80%9CProgressive+Bulgaria%E2%80%9D+Surges+Ahead+in+April+Elections+Projection,+GERB+Trails+Far+Behind)

[(Metaculus) Will any party or coalition other than GERB-SDS or PP-DB win the most seats in Bulgaria's April 2026 parliamentary election before May 1, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43055)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if the official final result shows that a party or coalition other than GERB-SDS or PP-DB won the most seats in Bulgaria's April 2026 parliamentary election, using the Central Election Commission page at https://www.cik.bg/ as the primary source. Otherwise, this question resolves to no.
fine_print: If seat counts tie, use the official ordering or coalition designation used by the election authority. Candidate Sources: Wikipedia election page if available before resolution; credible sources per https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions
